arts.ttl: GRAEME SOUNESS: 'Unstoppable' Kevin De Bruyne has left his mark on the English game... and the one boss who should beat fabulous Nuno Espirito Santo to Manager of the Season prize
arts.sum: GRAEME SOUNESS: I believe he was the best midfield player in the Premier League for a good five years. In time, supporters will reflect on him saying: 'You should have seen him play'.

arts.ttl: Why Unai Emery refused to talk about Boubacar Kamara contract talks
arts.sum: Unai Emery was reluctant to comment on Aston Villa’s contract talks with Boubacar Kamara as he wants all of his players to be fully focused on the crucial games ahead. Aston Villa are in talks with Boubacar Kamara over signing a new contract

Fixtures Chelsea U21 Vs. Aston Villa U21 - Fri, Apr 4th 2025, 19:00 Aston Villa fell to defeat on the road in Premier League 2 on Friday night, losing 4-1 to Chelsea at Stamford Bridge. Two goals in each half – from Ishe Samuels-Smith and Shumaira Mheuka, and then Donnell McNeilly and Brodi Hughes – meant the damage was done for Josep Gombau’s side with 20 minutes to go. Substitute Rory Wilson did grab a late consolation for the Young Lions in the closing stages, but they were unable to take anything more in their final away league outing of the campaign. Chelsea started fast and ferociously in West London, taking the lead with less than a minute on the clock. Genesis Antwi drove through the heart of the visiting defence with poise and purpose, pulling back inside the area for Samuels-Smith who calmly stroked into the bottom corner. Villa responded, Mason Cotcher forcing a smart stop from Lucas Bergstrom having been found by George Hemmings, but the Blues were much the aggressors, and had doubled their advantage with the game just 10 minutes old. Antwi again caused the Villans problems, this time down Chelsea’s right-hand side, darting to the byline and dinking a ball in which was only partially cleared as far as Mheuka. The forward took a touch to steady himself and blasted into the back of the net. Gombau’s charges struggled to hold possession for any sustained period throughout, and the hosts kept coming as they looked for a third before half-time. Four minutes after adding their second, Sam Proctor had to be alert to keep out McNeilly from inside the area. And Villa had their goalkeeper to thank again just after the half-hour, as he stood firm and showed impressive reflexes to deny Mheuka what looked a certain second, as he crashed a powerful header towards goal. The dangerous Mheuka threatened again as the break approached, with Thierry Katsukunya this time required to hack the ball off the line, as the referee brought the opening period to a close with Villa two goals down. The beginning of the second half brought a renewed composure from the visiting side, as they searched for a way back into the game, but the Blues soon regained their foothold as they looked to stretch their advantage beyond reach. Within 10 minutes of the restart, Proctor was again tested, first denying Harrison Murray-Campbell’s looping header from a corner, before moments later producing a fine double stop to keep substitute Ronnie Stutter and Sam Rak-Sakyi at bay. But it wouldn’t take much longer for the West London outfit to add a third, as a free-kick was dinked to the far post allowing McNeilly to lash past Proctor on the volley. And six minutes later, things would go from bad to worse for Villa. Kiano Dyer showed good awareness to drift away from the visiting defence and into a shooting position on the edge of the area. His effort deflected high into the air, and Hughes beat Proctor to the ball to head home a fourth. The Villans fought until the end, looking for a way back into the encounter, and were rewarded for their efforts three minutes from time, Wilson breaking clear inside the area and finishing neatly past substitute goalkeeper Luke Campbell. But that was about as good as it got for the away side, with Wilson’s effort proving little more than a consolation, as Villa left the capital empty handed. Upcoming Fixtures

arts.ttl: 'I think': Roy Keane and Gary Neville disagree as Aston Villa vs Nottingham Forest prediction is made
Matchday Guide: Aston Villa v Nottingham Forest
arts.sum: Fixtures Aston Villa Vs. Nottingham Forest - Sat, Apr 5th 2025, 17:30 Aston Villa go in search of a seventh-straight victory in all competitions on Saturday, with Nottingham Forest the visitors to Villa Park. Unai Emery's side host the top-four hopefuls looking to close the gap on the Champions League spots, having won their last two in the Premier League. Here's everything you need to know ahead of the encounter to enjoy your visit on matchday... Getting to the game Click on the sections below to learn more about tickets, Villa Park and transport to the stadium. Mobile Tickets Villa Park is now fully NFC ticketing enabled for supporters to access the stadium. Near-Field Communication (NFC) enables fans to enter the stadium via Apple or Google Wallet. Supporters can now also access their match-by-match tickets and enter the stadium directly via the Aston Villa app. Simply log into your Digital Account in the app and your tickets will appear under the ‘Tickets’ section. Supporters must update their app to the latest version to ensure in-app ticketing works without issue. Tickets A limited number of general admission tickets for the match remain. Book online here. The best way to secure tickets for future matches at Villa Park is via a Membership. Get Matchday Ready Ahead of the match at Villa Park, the club would like to advise supporters of the following ticketing information to ensure everyone enjoys their visit to our stadium. Fans will need to provide one of the two specified ticket types to gain entry to the stadium - access tickets in our official Aston Villa mobile app or download to Apple/Google Wallet for NFC entry. Hospitality and Premium Experiences The club has a range of hospitality and GA+ areas to complement our existing offer. Check out all the information and book online here. Hospitality bookings for our upcoming home matches can be made over the phone on 0333 323 1982 (option 1) or by emailing hospitality@avfc.co.uk. Under-3s Policy Many supporters may not be aware of the policy regarding under-3s, so we are reminding supporters that under-3s are only permitted to be in the Family areas in the Trinity Road stand at Villa Park and must have a match ticket. Further information can be found here Aston Villa Ticketing (avfc.co.uk) Visiting Villa Park It's important that supporters take steps to plan their visit to the stadium. Turnstiles open at 4pm, so make sure you arrive at the stadium in plenty of time ahead of kick-off to ensure smooth entry. Please note, access to stiles is maintained during the first half and will close once the half-time whistle is blown. However you're planning on getting to the match, take a look at our Travel & Parking guide before leaving home. A traffic exclusion zone will be in operation around the stadium, meaning there will be less traffic on Trinity Road and Witton Lane from up to two hours before the match starts. The Holte Pub will open three hours prior to kick-off and close two hours after full-time. Key Travel Information There will be changes to the junction at Queensbridge Road and Kings Heath High Street (A435) as part of the ongoing development of the new Kings Heath Station. For detailed information please click here. There are temporary changes to the bus stops at Moor Street to the following services: National Express West Midlands 35, 17, 60, X1 & X2 and National Express West Midlands/Diamond 50. For detailed information, please visit here. Please plan ahead and check train timetables for direct services or travel earlier for less busy services. Please visit Transport for West Midlands’ Journey Planner or the National Rail journey planner here. For real time traffic updates, follow @wmroads on X.  Our dedicated city centre shuttle bus service, the AV82, will operate for the match. For more information on the AV82, visit here. Our shuttle bus to and from Tamworth, the AV1, will also be in operation. For more information, visit here. You can also pre-book your ticket for the AV1 bus here. To help you plan your journey by public transport, please visit How to get to Villa Park. Supporters are advised that the supply of car parking around Villa Park is limited. Supporters can pre-book their car parking space via either of the links below. www.astonvillaparking.co.uk www.countrywidemanagementsolutions.co.uk We would strongly encourage all supporters who have not pre-booked their car parking to travel to Villa Park by alternative modes. City Centre and Tamworth Shuttle Buses Aston Villa is delighted to confirm to supporters that our AV82 city centre shuttle bus service will continue to run on matchdays this season. This follows a successful trial of the service at the back end of last season. The service will be in operation for all men’s home matches in the Premier League, UEFA Champions League and all domestic cup competitions, helping supporters travel more easily to and from Villa Park. Run by Stagecoach, the AV82 shuttle picks supporters up from outside Birmingham New Street Station on Dudley Street, running directly to Villa Park. Buses run every 15 minutes, starting three hours before kick-off for every home fixture. Services run back to Birmingham New Street at regular intervals for up to 90 minutes after the final whistle of every game, with services departing from Grosvenor Road, on the walking route towards Aston Station. Traffic signals have been optimised around Villa Park to help improve flow of traffic away from the ground. This should assist with journey times for our AV82 city centre shuttle bus. The club also has a dedicated bus service, AV1, that runs to Villa Park from Tamworth on matchday. You can purchase tickets here. More information is available here. Cycle Parking Covered and secure cycle parking is available a short walk away from Villa Park at our Inner City Academy, Brook Vale, on Witton Road. Accessibility information Supporters with accessibility requirements travelling to Villa Park by rail are advised to look out for the accessibility signage on both platforms at Witton Station. Station staff will be able to help you if you wait in the designated areas. More information is available here. For any supporters with additional mobility needs who have questions about travel to and from Villa Park on a matchday, please contact accessibility@avfc.co.uk. Pre-match activities Click on the sections below to learn more about activities taking place at Villa Park before kick-off. Enjoy the Fan Zone Villa Park's new Fan Zone is again open on matchday, located in the former Players' Car Park. The new space opens its doors at 2.30pm, with supporters able to enjoy a bar area and food. The Fan Zone is also in prime position to watch player arrivals from 4pm. We also have entertainment taking place on the stage opposite Trinity Reception, including live music from Quadrox + Dhol and drums from Punjabi Villans! We want to see your experience of matchday at Villa Park! Post your photos and tag #UTV and you could be featured on the big screens at the stadium! Supporters are advised that the R44 accessible entrance will be closed for a period of time. Family Fun Zone Work is soon to begin on the transformation of the existing Academy building at Villa Park. Unfortunately, due to the planned works, the Academy building is not operational to the extent to which we can safely open the Family Fun Zone. The club are working on alternate arrangements to enable a continuation of some of the family-friendly pre-match activities. Updates will follow in the coming weeks. Food and Beverage Villa Park offers a wide range of cuisine and beverages on a matchday. Outside the stadium, several street food trucks will sit alongside a bar serving alcoholic and soft drinks from 2.30pm. Supporters are reminded that Aston Villa adopts a one-cup system at Villa Park, in our continued environmental sustainability efforts. Cashless Stadium Please note, Villa Park will be cashless to help ensure safe social distancing practices and minimise contact at all points of sale. This includes the Villa Store, with card payments widely accepted in and around the stadium. The only exception is programme kiosks outside the ground, which will still accept cash. In-stadia betting kiosks will also be in operation for this fixture and such service will be provided on a cashless basis. North Grounds Update Aston Villa Football Club has announced its plans for a bold regeneration project at Villa Park that will build on the Club’s 150-year heritage, while also creating the fan experience zone of the future. Expected to go live Christmas 2025, the new project includes the launch of The Warehouse, a new multi-use venue set to become a centrepiece of the matchday experience and a hub for year-round entertainment, along with a new ticketing office, improved plaza in the North Grounds, and expanded footprint of the club shop. Admission to The Warehouse will be free for ticketed supporters on matchdays, when the venue becomes the largest beer hall in the Premier League – a vibrant space to connect, enjoy a pint, and soak in pre-match excitement with state-of-the-art LED lights and flat-screen TVs. Beyond matchdays, The Warehouse will transform into the Midlands’ premier concert venue and event space. The 3,500-seater venue will host world-class performances, from live music and sporting events to community activities, creating a versatile space for audiences of all kinds. Designed to bring the Aston and Birmingham communities together, it will also serve as a platform for local and global talent, making an impact far beyond Villa Park. More information here. Matchday Programme The latest edition of the Villa News & Record will be available to purchase at Saturday's match! Fans can continue their sticker album collection, while the programme is also packed with features and news from around the club. Supporters will be able to purchase copies from sellers at Villa Park on matchday, while they're also available to purchase in the club shop and online here. And, the team are back at Villa Park, meaning it's time to get hunting for the remaining AV150 Digital Collectibles with socios.com. Remember to buy a matchday programme, with details above, find the AV150 page and scan the QR code to collect your exclusive Nottingham Forest AV150 Digital Collectible. There's also the chance to enter some exclusive competitions, so get hunting! T&Cs apply, 18+. Villa Store Click on the sections below to learn about merchandise points around the stadium. Store Opening Hours There are a number of places where supporters can shop for Aston Villa merchandise on a matchday, including the upgraded Villa Store. Below you'll find the opening times for each shop around the ground... Villa Store: 9.30am-5.30pm and 6.30pm-8.30pm Holte Store: 2.30pm-5.30pm and 6.30pm-8.30pm 82 Lounge: 2.30pm-5.30pm and 6.30pm-8.30pm Lower Grounds: 2.30pm-5.30pm and 6.30pm-8.30pm Trinity Store: 4pm until the end of half-time Retail offer - all 2024/25 shirts from £30! Those visiting Villa Park on Saturday will be able to take advantage of an exciting offer of all 2024/25 replica shirts being available for just £30 (kids) and £35 (adults)! This deal is redeemable in store at Villa Park ONLY, with supporters able to get their hands on a replica shirt from this memorable season, ahead of huge clashes in the Champions League and FA Cup. During the game Click on the sections below to learn more about events that take place during the match. See it...hear it...report it! If you witness discriminatory behaviour at a match or around a stadium, please report it to the police or a steward. Supporters at Villa Park can text REPORT to 60060 along with the location of the incident and details of what you have witnessed. The text line also includes the reporting of any safeguarding concerns. The text service will be supported by our security team and West Midlands Police. Each text costs one standard message at your network rate. The club may share your details with West Midlands Police if further information is required to assist with a criminal prosecution. All-seater Stadium We would like to remind supporters that Villa Park is an all-seater stadium. In all areas of Villa Park, we have children, elderly fans and those with medical conditions who are unable to see or watch the match if supporters stand up in front of them. Please be mindful of this at all times and thank you for your cooperation. Fan Engagement Click the sections below to learn about how you can get involved on matchday and our fan loyalty programme, Villa Rewards. Get Involved New for 2024/25, the ‘Villa Predictor’ game will give fans the opportunity to win a £500 store voucher if they correctly predict the outcome of the game. T&Cs apply. We want to know about your matchday experience at Villa Park. Tweet us using #UTV and you could feature on the big screens at the stadium! Pride Rewards The Pride Rewards Portal is back up and running. Log in here. AV150 Legacy Wall Aston Villa is excited to reveal a new AV150 Legacy Wall at Villa Park as part of our 150th anniversary celebrations. Supporters have the opportunity to become part of our iconic home, wherever you are in the world, by purchasing your very own personalised plaque at Villa Park. Our Legacy Wall is a place where you, your friends and family can show your support for the club, celebrate births, marriages, anniversaries or pay tribute to loved ones. For more information, click here. AV150 Digital Collectibles The team are back at Villa Park, meaning it's time to get hunting for the remaining AV150 Digital Collectibles with socios.com. Remember to buy a matchday programme, with details above, find the AV150 page and scan the QR code to collect your exclusive Nottingham Forest AV150 Digital Collectible. There's also the chance to enter some exclusive competitions, so get hunting! For full details on AV150 Digital Collectibles, click here. T&Cs apply, 18+.
